queries.name: Customer Segment Revenue Analysis with Purchase Behavior
queries.pain_point: Need to understand which customer segments drive the most revenue and their purchasing patterns
queries.query:
FROM purchase_transactions
| LOOKUP JOIN customers ON customer_id
| STATS total_revenue = SUM(purchase_amount), 
        avg_transaction = AVG(purchase_amount), 
        transaction_count = COUNT(*), 
        unique_customers = COUNT_DISTINCT(customer_id) 
  BY customers.customer_segment, purchase_channel
| EVAL revenue_per_customer = total_revenue / unique_customers
| SORT total_revenue DESC
| LIMIT 20
queries.description: Analyzes revenue by customer segment and purchase channel, calculating key metrics like average transaction value and revenue per customer to identify high-value segments

queries.name: Product Category Performance with Customer Demographics
queries.pain_point: Understanding which products are purchased by specific demographic groups for targeted marketing
queries.query:
FROM purchase_transactions
| LOOKUP JOIN products ON product_id
| LOOKUP JOIN customers ON customer_id
| WHERE customers.demographic_age_group IS NOT NULL
| STATS total_units = SUM(quantity), 
        total_revenue = SUM(purchase_amount), 
        avg_basket_size = AVG(quantity) 
  BY products.product_category, customers.demographic_age_group
| EVAL revenue_per_unit = total_revenue / total_units
| SORT total_revenue DESC
| LIMIT 30
queries.description: Identifies which product categories resonate with specific age demographics, enabling targeted marketing campaigns and inventory optimization

queries.name: High-Value Customer Purchase Pattern Comparison
queries.pain_point: Need to compare individual customer purchase behavior against their segment average to identify upsell opportunities
queries.query:
FROM purchase_transactions
| LOOKUP JOIN customers ON customer_id
| WHERE customers.lifetime_value > 1000
| INLINESTATS segment_avg_purchase = AVG(purchase_amount) BY customers.customer_segment
| EVAL purchase_vs_segment_avg = purchase_amount - segment_avg_purchase
| EVAL performance_ratio = purchase_amount / segment_avg_purchase
| WHERE performance_ratio > 1.5
| SORT purchase_vs_segment_avg DESC
| KEEP customer_id, customers.customer_name, customers.customer_segment, purchase_amount, segment_avg_purchase, purchase_vs_segment_avg, performance_ratio
| LIMIT 50
queries.description: Identifies high-value customers whose individual purchases significantly exceed their segment average, revealing upsell and VIP engagement opportunities

queries.name: Semantic Product Discovery for Customer Buying Patterns
queries.pain_point: Finding customers who buy specific product types using natural language descriptions rather than rigid category filters
queries.query:
FROM purchase_transactions
| LOOKUP JOIN products ON product_id
| LOOKUP JOIN customers ON customer_id
| WHERE SEMANTIC(products.product_description, "premium organic health wellness products")
| STATS total_spent = SUM(purchase_amount), 
        purchase_frequency = COUNT(*), 
        avg_order_value = AVG(purchase_amount) 
  BY customer_id, customers.customer_name, customers.customer_segment
| WHERE purchase_frequency >= 3
| SORT total_spent DESC
| LIMIT 25
queries.description: Uses semantic search to identify customers who frequently purchase specific product types described in natural language, enabling precise customer profiling for targeted campaigns

queries.name: Multi-Channel Customer Engagement Dashboard with Parameterization
queries.pain_point: Need a flexible view of customer engagement across online and in-store channels for different time periods
queries.query:
FROM purchase_transactions
| WHERE @timestamp >= NOW() - ?time_range
| LOOKUP JOIN customers ON customer_id
| WHERE customers.customer_segment == ?segment
| FORK
  (STATS total_revenue = SUM(purchase_amount), 
         transaction_count = COUNT(*) 
   BY purchase_channel)
  (EVAL day = DATE_TRUNC(1 day, @timestamp) 
   | STATS daily_revenue = SUM(purchase_amount) BY day 
   | SORT day DESC 
   | LIMIT 30)
  (WHERE purchase_channel == "online" 
   | STATS online_customers = COUNT_DISTINCT(customer_id), 
          online_revenue = SUM(purchase_amount))
  (WHERE purchase_channel == "in-store" 
   | STATS store_customers = COUNT_DISTINCT(customer_id), 
          store_revenue = SUM(purchase_amount))
queries.description: Provides a comprehensive multi-dimensional view of customer engagement across channels with flexible time ranges and segment filtering for market research analysis
